title: TCP Connections, Accept Loops, Half-Closes, Deadlines, and Failure · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs tcp connections, accept loops, half-closes, deadlines, and failure from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the bounded TCP connection lifecycle state machine. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded TCP connection lifecycle state machine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain ordered reliable byte-stream delivery while accounting for segmentation, retransmission, flow control, congestion, and missing message boundaries.
Assign listener, accepted connection, goroutine, buffer, deadline, and close ownership with bounded accept-error handling.
Set and refresh absolute read and write deadlines by protocol phase and classify timeout evidence without assuming cancellation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Tcp Reliable Stream Model
steps.instruction:
Order the Tcp Reliable Stream Model evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Tcp Reliable Stream Model.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Tcp Reliable Stream Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Tcp Reliable Stream Model, keep Tcp Reliable Stream Model connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A review protocol team handles case 3577 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify connection, deadline, completion, and close state without inventing message boundaries; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain ordered reliable byte-stream delivery while accounting for segmentation, retransmission, flow control, congestion, and missing message boundaries.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Tcp Reliable Stream Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
